purpose: Machine-readable maintainer roadmap, review-lane map, readiness estimates, and validation gates for the COBOL converter.

estimate_policy.meaning: Percentages are engineering readiness estimates, not certification claims.

split_policy.default_protocol: One implementer lane, one spec critic lane, one edge-case critic lane, then final integrator review.

when_to_split.rule: Split when tasks touch different compiler/runtime layers and can be validated by separate focused tests.
Split oracle/golden-behavior work away from implementation work so the oracle agent is not biased by implementation choices.
Split OS file, dynamic CALL, generated path, and runtime config work into a security/red-team lane.
Split codec correctness from generated-program integration; packed-decimal core and compiler use of packed fields fail differently.

when_not_to_split.rule: Do not let multiple implementers edit the same parser boundary/invariant in parallel.
Do not split a refactor that requires one coherent AST/IR contract across syntax, sema, and codegen.

forbidden_outcomes: unsupported executable statement lowered to VmProcedureOp::Noop
new partial feature without migration-capability-matrix.json update
oracle fixture that silently skips when it is meant to be required in CI

areas.name: COMP-3 packed decimal core
criticism: Codec maturity is higher than compiler integration maturity.
Sign-nibble policy, invalid data policy, lossless round trips, and strict precision paths must remain one shared implementation.
areas.dashboard_signal: Strong library core; do not treat this as proof that generated packed-field arithmetic is complete.

areas.name: COMP-3 inside generated COBOL programs
criticism: Generated accessors and fixed scalar MOVE/COMPUTE writes exist, but comparison, sorting, CALL edge cases, and invalid-data policy need broader oracle coverage.
COMP-3 SORT keys now decode numerically; binary/native/float SORT keys remain fail-closed and must not silently compare bytewise.
areas.dashboard_signal: Usable for covered access/display/MOVE/COMPUTE shapes; not complete for all packed arithmetic semantics.

areas.name: Source and COPY normalization
criticism: Source corruption before syntax parsing invalidates every downstream result.
Fixed continuation, COPY REPLACING, nested directives, and literal preservation need hostile fixtures.
areas.dashboard_signal: Basic use works; migration trust needs a token/source-map-aware preprocessor.

areas.name: Syntax and procedure parsing
criticism: Token-slice nested parsing is a real improvement, but sentence-level control flow is still missing.
NEXT SENTENCE remains blocked until period-scope CFG exists.
areas.dashboard_signal: Parser is safer than before; still not a full COBOL statement/sentence grammar.

areas.name: Semantic analysis and IR
criticism: The dangerous failures are semantic: aliases, ODO, REDEFINES, RENAMES, category conversion, and control-flow facts.
IR must keep becoming typed and executable rather than carrying raw COBOL fragments.
areas.dashboard_signal: Good blocking and reference work; aliasing and dynamic layout remain major migration risks.

areas.name: Codegen and VM
criticism: VM feature count is rising, but platform semantics and checkpoint/overlay interactions are not complete.
Generated Noop fallbacks must stay limited to explicit CONTINUE and proven zero-field MOVE CORRESPONDING.
areas.dashboard_signal: Strong enough for current subset; not a platform-accurate COBOL runtime yet.

areas.name: File and OS runtime
criticism: Fixed sequential is usable, but real migration needs VSAM/indexed/relative/tape/locking/disposition behavior.
OS file handles, paths, external content, and checkpoint state need security and restart scrutiny.
areas.dashboard_signal: Current runtime is a fixed sequential batch slice, not a mainframe file platform.

areas.name: Oracle validation
criticism: Oracle coverage is a seed, not a certification suite.
Every feature promotion should require observable behavior fixtures, not just generated-code substring checks.
areas.dashboard_signal: Useful for regression confidence; too small for migration trust.

areas.name: Full mainframe and ABYSS compatibility
criticism: The current VM has pieces of the chaos model, but overlays, tape, robust RERUN, platform collation, and full restart state are missing.
ABYSS work should be split only after the compiler subset has hard semantic gates.
areas.dashboard_signal: Some ABYSS hazards are modeled; full resurrection is still early-stage.

agent_lanes.name: Syntax/Parser Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: tokens.join(" ") reparsing in nested imperative lists

agent_lanes.name: Sema/IR Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: raw COBOL strings in executable IR for supported verbs

agent_lanes.name: Codegen/VM Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: unsupported executable statement lowered as Noop
VM state mutation without checkpoint consideration

agent_lanes.name: Runtime/File Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: file status behavior without oracle or exact smoke evidence

agent_lanes.name: Oracle Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: oracle fixture with no clear observable

agent_lanes.name: Security/Red-Team Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: generated file overwrite outside output root
dynamic call target accepted without explicit binding

agent_lanes.name: ABYSS Hazard Agent
forbidden_shortcuts: combined hazard fixture that hides accepted hazards

tasks.title: Implement sentence CFG and real NEXT SENTENCE semantics
split_when: Oracle-agent can independently build behavior fixtures for period-scope and NEXT SENTENCE.
Sema/codegen changes start only after syntax AST contract is reviewed.
acceptance: NEXT SENTENCE no longer reaches codegen as unsupported for supported sentence shapes.
Period-scope control flow has exact fixtures.
Existing token-slice nested branch tests remain green.

tasks.title: Broaden generated-program packed decimal arithmetic and comparisons
split_when: Oracle-agent owns observable COBOL arithmetic fixtures.
acceptance: Packed field arithmetic uses shared codec policy.
Invalid packed data has an explicit runtime policy.
Generated programs have exact oracle fixtures for signed, scaled, and overflow cases.

tasks.title: Build token/source-map-aware fixed/COPY preprocessor
split_when: Oracle-agent can independently add fixed/COPY fixtures.
acceptance: Fixed continuation and COPY REPLACING preserve literal boundaries.
Diagnostics retain enough normalized-source context for migration reports.
Free/fixed auto detection remains explicit-risk documented.

tasks.title: Harden OS file/runtime platform behavior
split_when: Security-agent owns path/config misuse and generated output safety.
Oracle-agent owns observable file status fixtures.
acceptance: OS file status behavior has exact tests.
Runtime config rejects unsafe or unsupported mappings predictably.
Checkpoint/file cursor behavior has an explicit documented policy.

tasks.title: Refactor ABYSS matrix to one hazard per fixture with exact diagnostics
split_when: Syntax/codegen critics verify that accepted hazards are not hidden behind unrelated blockers.
acceptance: Each ABYSS fixture declares exactly one primary hazard.
Diagnostics are asserted structurally, not by loose substring only.
Supported hazards have exact smoke or oracle coverage.
